feedback.text: Dear Sir or Madam, please add a clause to stop companies from making entertainment software, specifically video games, that we legally purchased unavailable to us without proper compensation. If I purchase a TV I can use it until it breaks, if I purchase a DVD I can watch it until the disk gives up. Shouldn't the same be true of video games unless it is a subscription, a purchase should mean ownership of that item, not a license that can be taken away without compensation or alternatives. A manufacturer can't come to you after two years and destroy the TV you purchased to make you buy a new one and yet this is now a common practice in video games. Please put a stop to this. Thank you in advance. Best Regards, Kevin Dicke
